scopeNote: Comprehensive product-relevant JVM troubleshooting scenarios diagnosable from the artifact types jtroubleshoot supports, including healthy controls, ambiguity cases, comparison or sequence regressions, and malformed or partial inputs.

strategies.summary: Generate internally consistent JVM artifacts from one controlled Java run. Use when timing alignment, causality, or binary JFR content matters.
Use sanitized real artifacts captured offline from controlled reproductions or trusted incidents. Use for legacy JDK logs, hs_err, pmap, kernel or Kubernetes signals, and unsafe native failures.
Use handcrafted or mutated artifacts for truncation, corruption, mixed ingest, rotated logs, and other parser or classifier edge conditions.
Combine simulator-generated JVM artifacts with captured or synthetic platform artifacts for cross-artifact correlation scenarios.
